name: Polish Pierogi
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the dough by mixing flour, eggs, sour cream, salt, and warm water a little at a time. Knead until firm and elastic. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
2. Prepare the potato filling by cooking and draining 3 medium potatoes. Mash them, then sauté with half a medium onion, 1/4 cup butter, salt, and pepper to taste.
3. Prepare the cheese filling by combining 1 lb dry cottage cheese, beaten eggs, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 cup butter. Mix until well combined.
4. Prepare the sauce by sautéing 1 large onion in 1/2 cup butter until golden brown.
5. Divide the dough into three parts. On a floured surface, roll each part to about 1/8-inch thick. Cut into 3-inch rounds.
6. Place a small spoonful of potato filling in the center of each round. Fold and press the edges together firmly to seal.
7. Drop the pierogi into simmering chicken bouillon with 1 teaspoon oil. Do not crowd; simmer gently for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
8. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain well.
9. Place drained pierogi in a casserole dish and pour sauce mixture over all. Garnish with mushrooms before serving.
